title: Wireless LAN Ecosystem
subtitle: Core Components Working Together in a Cisco Wireless Network

nodes.title: Wireless LAN Controller
nodes.desc: Centralized platform for WLAN management, security and RF optimization.
details: Centralized management of all Access Points.
Performs RF optimization and channel planning.
Supports seamless roaming across the wireless network.

nodes.title: Access Points
nodes.desc: Provide wireless coverage and connect clients to the network.
details: Connect wireless clients to enterprise resources.
Apply policies received from the controller.
Monitor RF conditions and client performance.

nodes.title: Wireless Clients
nodes.desc: Laptops, smartphones and tablets accessing WLAN services.
details: Access internal and external network resources.
Utilize voice, video and data applications.

nodes.title: AAA / RADIUS Server
nodes.desc: Handles authentication, authorization and accounting services.
details: Integrates with Active Directory and LDAP.

nodes.title: Network Infrastructure
nodes.desc: Switches and routers providing connectivity between APs and WLC.
details: Carries CAPWAP traffic between APs and WLC.

nodes.title: Guest Access Services
nodes.desc: Provides secure internet access for guest users and visitors.
details: Restricts access to internal enterprise resources.
Tracks guest sessions and internet usage.
